req.title: Create observation (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case

"""
Pytest tests for Observation creation requirements:
- Observation must be tied to a patient
- Observation must have a LOINC-coded code element
- Observations without code shall be rejected
- Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements
"""

import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
from decimal import Decimal
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
import uuid

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain model stubs – replace with your real imports
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# from your_package.models import Observation, Patient, Code, Coding, Quantity
# from your_package.services import ObservationService
# from your_package.exceptions import (
#     ValidationError, MissingCodeError, MissingPatientError,
#     InvalidTimestampError, InvalidQuantityError,
# )

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Inline stubs so the test file is self-contained and runnable as-is.
# Delete these once you wire in the real implementation.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class ValidationError(Exception):
    pass

class MissingCodeError(ValidationError):
    pass

class MissingPatientError(ValidationError):
    pass

class InvalidTimestampError(ValidationError):
    pass

class InvalidQuantityError(ValidationError):
    pass


LOINC_SYSTEM = "http://loinc.org"

DATETIME_MIN = datetime(1900, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)
DATETIME_MAX = datetime(2099, 12, 31, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

NUMERIC_MIN = Decimal("-1E+15")
NUMERIC_MAX = Decimal("1E+15")


class Coding:
    def __init__(self, system: str, code: str, display: str = ""):
        self.system = system
        self.code = code
        self.display = display


class Code:
    def __init__(self, codings: list[Coding], text: str = ""):
        self.codings = codings
        self.text = text


class Quantity:
    def __init__(self, value: Decimal, unit: str, system: str = "", code: str = ""):
        self.value = value
        self.unit = unit
        self.system = system
        self.code = code


class Patient:
    def __init__(self, patient_id: str):
        self.patient_id = patient_id


class Observation:
    """Stub Observation model – mirrors a FHIR R4 Observation."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        *,
        observation_id: str | None = None,
        patient: Patient | None = None,
        code: Code | None = None,
        effective_datetime: datetime | None = None,
        issued: datetime | None = None,
        value_quantity: Quantity | None = None,
        status: str = "final",
    ):
        self.observation_id = observation_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.patient = patient
        self.code = code
        self.effective_datetime = effective_datetime
        self.issued = issued
        self.value_quantity = value_quantity
        self.status = status


class ObservationService:
    """Stub service that enforces business rules."""

    def create(self, observation: Observation) -> Observation:
        self._validate(observation)
        observation.observation_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        return observation

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    # Validation helpers
    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
    def _validate(self, obs: Observation) -> None:
        self._require_patient(obs)
        self._require_loinc_code(obs)
        self._validate_timestamps(obs)
        self._validate_quantity(obs)

    def _require_patient(self, obs: Observation) -> None:
        if obs.patient is None or not obs.patient.patient_id:
            raise MissingPatientError("Observation must reference a patient.")

    def _require_loinc_code(self, obs: Observation) -> None:
        if obs.code is None:
            raise MissingCodeError("code element is required.")
        if not obs
